segments.p2-3-g150: С обезьяньим лицом = ‘with a monkey-like face’, where с + instrumental describes appearance; обезьяньим is the adjective ‘simian/monkey-like’.
segments.p2-3-g151: Всех пересмеивающий is a present active participle phrase ‘mocking everyone’ (пересмеивающий governs всех), followed by the noun глупец ‘fool’ — together ‘a fool who mocked everyone.’
segments.p2-3-g152: Злейший враг мой еще с низших классов is a packed phrase: злейший ‘arch-/most’ + враг мой ‘my enemy’ (word order inverted for emphasis), ещё с низших классов ‘already/since the lower classes’ (meaning ‘since our lower school years’ or ‘from the lower ranks’).
segments.p2-3-g153: Подлый is a single adjective here: ‘vile/base’ — a terse character judgment in a series of epithets.
segments.p2-3-g154: Дерзкий is another adjective in the series meaning ‘bold/insolent’ (character trait).
segments.p2-3-g155: Фанфаронишка is a pejorative noun ‘braggart’; и игравший is a past participle (‘and (one) playing/acting’) that modifies or extends the portrait. В самую щекотливую амбициозность uses в + accusative to mean ‘playing at/affecting the very most ticklish (sensitive) ambitions’ — i.e. courting delicate pretensions.
segments.p2-3-g156: Хотя introduces a contrasting concession ‘although’ that precedes the softening remark that follows.
segments.p2-3-g157: Разумеется is an adverb meaning ‘of course’ (a parenthetical comment preparing the following qualification).
segments.p2-3-g158: Трусишка в душе is the closing qualification: трусишка is a diminutive pejorative ‘little coward’ and в душе (‘in his soul/at heart’) means ‘at heart he was a coward.’
segments.p2-3-g338: 'О' is an exclamatory particle (poetic/archaised) here, equivalent to English 'Oh' expressing emotion; 'нет' is a simple negative interjection 'no'. The dash and exclamation mark mark a sudden, emphatic refusal or denial.
segments.p2-3-g339: Встрепенулся is a past, perfective, reflexive verb meaning 'started (up), stirred' and implies a sudden physical or emotional movement; the verb precedes 'он' (subject) here, a common inverted order in speech for vividness. Вдруг is an adverb 'suddenly' modifying the verb.
segments.p2-3-g340: То есть is a fixed phrase ('that is') used to rephrase or clarify what was just said; 'то' is the demonstrative 'that' and 'есть' is the present form of 'to be' used here in the idiom rather than as a full predicate.
segments.p2-3-g341: По правде is an adverbial expression meaning 'to tell the truth' or 'in truth'; 'по' here functions as 'according to' and правде is the noun 'truth' used idiomatically to mean 'honestly/really'.
segments.p2-3-g342: Да is a brief affirmative 'yes' confirming or softening the preceding clarification; placed after parenthetical commas it functions as a conversational confirmation.
segments.p2-3-g343: Видите ли is a conversational opener meaning 'you see' or 'you know'; 'видите' is second-person plural present of 'to see/understand' and 'ли' is the enclitic particle that gives the phrase its idiomatic, slightly tentative tone.
segments.p2-3-g344: Мне (dative) + надо (impersonal 'need/ought') plus the infinitive зайти expresses necessity for the speaker: literally 'to me it is necessary to drop by.' Еще 'still/yet' adds continuity/remaining obligation and бы is the conditional particle, producing 'I would still need to drop by.' Word order with the dative first is typical in Russian for impersonal necessity constructions.
segments.p2-3-g347: Тут is an adverb 'here/at this place' and недалеко is an adverb/predicative 'not far' (literally 'not far'), together forming a terse locative comment equivalent to 'it's not far from here' with the copula omitted.
segments.p2-3-g333: Он осекся is a past‑tense, reflexive verb form meaning 'fell silent' or 'stopped short' (masculine subject). и стал ходить combines стал (past of стать, here meaning 'began') with the infinitive ходить to indicate the start of a repeated/ongoing action ('began to walk/pace'). по комнате uses the preposition по + prepositional case (комнате) to mean 'around/throughout the room', and с + instrumental (с досадой) expresses manner ('with annoyance'); ещё modifies большой (еще большей = 'even greater') and agrees in gender/case/number with досадой (instrumental feminine).
segments.p2-3-g334: Шагая is a present adverbial participle (деепричастие) from шагать and means 'walking' or 'while/as he walked', indicating a simultaneous action to the main clause.
segments.p2-3-g335: он начал is past tense 'he began' (начал + infinitive). становиться на каблуки literally 'to stand on (one's) heels' — становиться here denotes taking a posture (infinitive used after начал). на + accusative/onto (каблуки) indicates movement/position 'onto the heels'. и при этом links a simultaneous consequence; при этом (literally 'at/by this') means 'in doing so' or 'and at the same time'. сильнее is the comparative adverb 'more/stronger' modifying топать, and топать (infinitive) means 'to stamp' (to stomp the foot).
segments.p2-3-g336: Я вас не задерживаю ли? is a negatively framed question: Я (subject) + вас (direct object 'you') + не (negation) + задерживаю (present tense 'I am detaining/holding up'); the enclitic ли at the end turns the clause into a question (often rhetorical). Word order is neutral but the enclitic emphasizes the interrogative.
segments.p2-3-g337: спросил я is past tense 'I asked' with inverted word order (verb before pronoun) for narrative emphasis. после governs the genitive (после двухминутного молчанья = 'after two minutes of silence'); двухминутного is a genitive singular adjective agreeing with молчанья (genitive of молчание, 'silence').
segments.p2-3-g360: Это is a demonstrative pronoun used as a dummy subject ('it'/'this') introducing a predicative clause. Ведь is a particle adding a confirming/reminding tone ('you know' / 'after all'). Недалеко is an adverb functioning as the predicate meaning 'not far' — the clause literally reads 'It (this) after all not-far.'
segments.p2-3-g363: Тут is an adverb meaning 'here' or 'at this spot' and opens an elliptical locative sentence. Два шага is a numeral + noun phrase where два requires the genitive singular шага; there is no explicit verb, so the phrase expresses distance/degree ('(it's) two steps').
segments.p2-3-g366: Повторял is the past tense, imperfective aspect, third-person singular masculine of повторять — here indicating he kept repeating (habitual/continuous past). The noun Симонов is the proper name/subject; the comma after it marks the clause break before the participial phrase.
segments.p2-3-g367: Провожая is a present adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'escorting/seeing off', indicating a concurrent action and governing the direct object меня (accusative 'me'). До + передней: до requires the genitive, so передней is genitive singular of передняя ('anteroom/front room'), meaning 'as far as the anteroom'. С + суетливым видом: с takes the instrumental, so суетливым видом (instrumental) means 'with a fussy/bustling appearance' and describes his manner while escorting.
segments.p2-3-g368: Который is a relative pronoun referring back to 'видом' (the 'air/appearance'). Ему is a dative experiencer ('to him'). Вовсе не is an emphatic negation meaning 'not at all.' Шел is the past tense, masculine singular form of идти used idiomatically here to mean 'to suit' or 'to become' (literal senses of идти include 'to go', but in clothing/manner contexts it means 'to suit').
segments.p2-3-g369: Так here is a discourse particle meaning 'so/then' used to introduce or confirm a plan. Завтра is the temporal adverb 'tomorrow.' В is the preposition used with time expressions ('at'/'on' in English), and пять часов is the numeral + noun where часов is the genitive plural form used with numbers (meaning 'five o'clock'). Ровно is an adverb meaning 'exactly' or 'sharp', modifying the time.
segments.p2-3-g276: "Мы" is the nominative plural subject 'we'; "вас" is the accusative (or genitive) object 'you'. The pronoun-object follows the subject here with a comma after it for a slight pause or emphasis.
segments.p2-3-g277: "может быть" is a parenthetical modal phrase meaning 'maybe' or 'perhaps'. Grammatically it is composed of the 3rd-person form "может" + infinitive "быть", but together they function as a hedge indicating possibility; the comma marks it off.
segments.p2-3-g278: "и" here is a connective/ emphatic particle ('and' / 'even') introducing the negated predicate; "совсем не" is the strong negation 'not at all' modifying the verb; "хотим" is 1st person plural present of "хотеть" 'to want'. The clause reads literally 'and (we) entirely not want' → 'we don't want (you) at all.'
segments.p2-3-g281: "Они" is the plural subject 'they'; "ушли" is the past tense, perfective plural of "уйти/уходить" meaning 'left' or 'went away'. The semicolon separates this finished action from the description that follows.
segments.p2-3-g282: Proper name in the nominative, marking the next subject/topic of the sentence; the comma prepares an attached adverbial participle clause.
segments.p2-3-g283: "уходя" is a деепричастие (adverbial participle) from "уходить", meaning 'while leaving' or 'as he was leaving'. It gives the temporal background for the main verb that follows and requires a comma.
segments.p2-3-g284: "мне" is a dative pronoun 'to me'; "совсем не" forms a strong negation 'not at all'; "поклонился" is past masculine singular perfective 'bowed' (here negated: 'didn't bow'). Word order (dative before verb) is normal for Russian and emphasizes the recipient of the (non-)action.
segments.p2-3-g285: Name as subject followed by the adverb "едва" ('hardly/barely') and the past verb "кивнул" ('nodded'). The sequence means 'Trudolyubov barely nodded.'
segments.p2-3-g286: "не глядя" is a negated adverbial participle meaning 'without looking' or 'not looking'. Placed after the verb it qualifies the manner of the nod.
segments.p2-3-g287: Proper name in nominative, marking the subject of the relative clause that follows.
segments.p2-3-g288: "с которым" is the relative phrase 'with whom' ("с" + instrumental "которым"); "я остался" is past tense 'I remained / I was left' ("остался" here conveys being left/remaining with someone); "с глазу на глаз" is an idiomatic adverbial phrase literally 'from eye to eye' meaning 'face to face' or 'one-on-one'. The whole relative clause: 'with whom I remained face to face.'
segments.p2-3-g289: "был" is past singular of 'to be' (he was); "в каком-то" = 'in some kind of' (indefinite qualifier); "досадливом недоумении" is a prepositional noun phrase 'an irritated/annoyed bewilderment' describing his state; "и" links the next action; "странно посмотрел на меня" = 'looked at me strangely' ("странно" adverb modifying "посмотрел", "на меня" the object 'at me').
segments.p2-3-g290: This whole segment is a coordinated negative statement. "Он" is the subject 'he'; "не садился" is the past tense imperfective negative ('didn't sit down' — imperfective emphasizes the action was not undertaken); "и" coordinates the clauses; "меня не приглашал" places the object 'me' before the negated past imperfective verb 'didn't invite' (i.e. he did not invite me). The double imperfective/past frames both omissions as actions he did not perform.
segments.p2-3-g299: "Я это," is a clipped, colloquial phrase: the pronoun Я (subject) followed by the demonstrative/placeholder это functioning as a discourse filler ('this/that' or 'just so you know'); the comma marks a pause before the purpose clause.
segments.p2-3-g300: чтоб + infinitive (чтоб знать) expresses purpose 'in order to know/so as to know'; верно here modifies знать with the meaning 'for sure / certainly' (know for certain). The comma closes the parenthetical purpose phrase.
segments.p2-3-g301: The dash introduces the speech tag. пробормотал is past tense, perfective, meaning 'muttered/mumbled'; the word order пробормотал он places the verb before the pronoun for slight emphasis. сконфузившись is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) 'having become embarrassed' that attaches to the subject (he), describing the attendant circumstance.
segments.p2-3-g302: Я вспыхнул is a simple past declarative: вспыхнул is perfective past (masculine singular) meaning an abrupt emotional reaction ('I flushed/blushed/got angry'); Я is the explicit subject.
segments.p2-3-g303: но is the coordinating conjunction 'but/however' introducing a contrast with the previous clause.
segments.p2-3-g304: вспыхивая is the imperfective gerund (деепричастие) meaning 'while/flushing' or 'as I was flushing', indicating a simultaneous, ongoing action that sets the circumstance for the main verb вспомнил that follows.
segments.p2-3-g305: вспомнил is past perfective 'I remembered/recalled'; the subject is omitted here but understood as the same 'I'. It is the main verb of the clause begun by но.
segments.p2-3-g306: The clause is introduced by the complementizer что ('that'). с + незапамятных времён is an idiomatic prepositional phrase 'since time immemorial' (с = 'since/from'); должен был + dative (Симонову) + amount (пятнадцать рублей) uses должен был in the past to mean 'was due/owed' (i.e. 'I had owed Simonov fifteen rubles'). The dative marks the creditor (to Simonov).
segments.p2-3-g307: чего is a genitive form of что used here as a relative/referring particle 'which' or 'of which' pointing back to the preceding clause (the fact of owing fifteen rubles).
segments.p2-3-g308: впрочем is a parenthetical adverb 'however / nevertheless / incidentally' that softens or qualifies the following statement.
segments.p2-3-g309: и не забывал никогда is a negated imperfective past 'and I never forgot' (literally 'and did not forget ever'); and (и) adds slight emphasis/continuation. The imperfective забывал indicates a continuing/habitual mental state in the past.
segments.p2-3-g310: но и не отдавал никогда is the contrasting clause 'but nor did I ever give/pay it back'. но sets the contrast, и adds emphasis ('not even'), не + отдавал (imperfective past) indicates habitual failure to repay, and никогда = 'never'.
segments.p2-3-g311: Согласитесь is a 2nd-person plural (or polite) imperative or future-of-command form 'agree' / 'you must admit'; сами is an emphatic reflexive/adverb meaning 'yourself/yourselves', strengthening the appeal to the addressee.
segments.p2-3-g312: Симонов is simply the addressee's surname in vocative position (address), set off by commas.
segments.p2-3-g313: что introduces the content clause 'that...'; я не мог знать is past-modal: мог (past of мочь) + infinitive знать expresses inability in the past—'I could not / could not have known'; the negation не makes it 'could not'.
segments.p2-3-g314: входя is a present gerund (деепричастие) 'entering' meaning 'on entering / when entering'; сюда is a directional adverb 'here / to here' specifying the place — together they give the circumstance ('when I came in').
segments.p2-3-g317: и мне очень досадно is an impersonal construction meaning 'and it is very annoying to me' or 'I'm very annoyed'; мне is the dative experiencer ('to me'), досадно is a predicative adjective/short-form adjective meaning 'annoying/regrettable', and очень intensifies it.
segments.p2-3-g318: что я забыл is a subordinate clause introduced by что ('that') with the subject я and past tense verb забыл 'forgot' serving as the content of what is annoying to the speaker.
segments.p2-3-g350: Прибавил — past tense, perfective verb (he added/said), with the subject pronoun он following the verb by inversion for narrative emphasis. Какие‑то is an indefinite modifier ('some/some kind of') that here vaguely qualifies omitted words (i.e. 'something' he added). Извиняющимся голосом is an instrumental phrase ('in an apologetic voice') using the present participle извиняющимся as an adjective; и отчасти стыдясь is a de‑eptichastie (gerund) 'being/feeling partly ashamed' that comments on the manner of adding.
segments.p2-3-g351: Ах is an interjection expressing surprise or strong feeling; it stands alone as an exclamatory particle.
segments.p2-3-g352: Боже мой is a vocative/exclamatory phrase: боже is the vocative form of бог used here as 'O God' or 'God!', and мой is the possessive 'my' — together an idiomatic exclamation 'my God!'.
segments.p2-3-g353: Что же is an emphatic/exclamatory phrase: что functions as an exclamatory 'what/why' and же adds emphasis. Вы is the 2nd‑person plural/formal pronoun. Не is the negation particle; together with the verb it forms the rhetorical negative question ("why don't you..."). The written sка-же-те represents the verb скажете split into syllables (showing broken/stammering speech); grammatically it is the 2nd person plural future/indicative form (or used rhetorically as an exhortation), and the hyphenation indicates interrupted or emphatic delivery rather than separate words.
segments.p2-3-g354: Вскрикнул is past tense, perfective, masculine singular — 'he cried out/shouted'; the subject pronoun я follows the verb by inversion common in narration, so 'вскрикнул я' = 'I cried out.'
segments.p2-3-g355: Схватив is a perfective verbal adverb (деепричастие) meaning 'having grabbed' or 'snatching (up)', marking a secondary action contemporaneous with the main verb; фуражку is the direct object in the accusative ('my peaked cap').
segments.p2-3-g356: С + удивительно introduces a instrumental construction describing manner: с 'with' plus удивительно, an adverb ('surprisingly'/'astonishingly'), which modifies the following adjective ('развязным') and signals the adverbial degree of the quality that follows.
segments.p2-3-g357: Впрочем is a parenthetical particle meaning 'however' or 'though', here interrupting the phrase to downplay or qualify the preceding adverb.
segments.p2-3-g358: Развязным видом is an instrumental noun phrase 'with an insolent/brazen appearance'; развязным is an adjective in instrumental singular agreeing with видом (instrumental of вид), together indicating the speaker's manner/air.
segments.p2-3-g359: Бог знает is an idiomatic parenthetical 'God knows' (бог 'God' + знает 'knows'); откуда is an adverb 'from where/whence' and налетевшим is a past active participle in the instrumental (agreeing with видом) meaning 'having swooped in/come on suddenly' — together: 'a ... air that, God knows from where, had swooped in.'
segments.p2-3-g370: The verb 'крикнул' is past tense, masculine singular, indicating a single shout; the dash marks reported speech. 'Он' is the subject pronoun 'he' placed after the verb for a conversational, slightly abrupt word order. 'Мне' is the dative 'to me' showing the addressee. 'На лестницу' is an adverbial phrase with 'на' + accusative used here to indicate the stairway as the location/source of the shout (colloquially rendered in English as 'from the stairs' or 'down the stairs'); the colon that follows signals that what comes next is the content or reason for the shout.
segments.p2-3-g371: 'Очень уж' is a double intensifier (очень = 'very', уж adding emphasis/colloquial force). 'Он был доволен' is a past-tense clause with the past auxiliary 'был' plus the short-form adjective 'доволен' functioning as the predicate ('he was pleased'); the adverbial intensifier precedes the subject for extra emphasis on how pleased he was.
segments.p2-3-g372: 'Что' here is the complementizer 'that' introducing the content of his pleasure. 'Я' is the first-person subject. 'Ухожу' is the present-tense (imperfective) of 'уходить' used in the subordinate clause to mean 'I am leaving' (in English usually rendered past in sequence-of-tenses: 'I was leaving' since the matrix verb is past). The infinitive/perfective choice would change nuance; here the present indicates the ongoing action at the time.
segments.p2-3-g373: 'Я же' places contrastive emphasis on the speaker ('I, on the other hand' / 'I, however'). 'Был' is the past tense of 'to be'. 'В бешенстве' is a fixed expression meaning 'in a fury' or 'furious' (preposition 'в' + noun in prepositional form), so the whole clause states a past emotional state with contrastive emphasis.
segments.p2-3-g374: 'Ведь' is an emphatic particle ('after all' / 'you know') introducing a justification or exclamation. 'Дернуло' is an impersonal past-neuter form of 'дёрнуть' used colloquially to mean 'a sudden impulse seized (me)' or 'I got a sudden tug/urge' — no explicit grammatical subject is needed. The trailing 'же' adds further emphasis (colloquial intensifier).
segments.p2-3-g375: The repetition 'дернуло же' repeats the impersonal past to emphasize the involuntary impulse; the infinitive 'выскочить' is the complement describing what the impulse was (to jump/bolt out). 'Же' again functions as an intensifier attached to the clause. Together they render 'I suddenly felt the urge to jump/bolt out.'
segments.p2-3-g376: 'Скрежетал' is past-tense, imperfective, describing repeated or continuous action 'was grinding/gnashing'; here it means 'gnashed' (one's teeth). 'Я' is the subject. 'Зубами' is the instrumental plural ('with my teeth') used adverbially to express how he acted: 'gnashing his teeth.'
segments.p2-3-g377: 'Шагая' is a gerund (деепричастие) meaning 'while stepping / walking' and indicates a concurrent action. 'По' + dative 'улице' means 'along the street' or 'down the street' — a typical Russian way to indicate movement along a thoroughfare.
segments.p2-3-g378: 'И' here functions as an intensifying 'even/and' preceding the target of his anger. 'Этакому' is the demonstrative in dative singular with a pejorative tone ('to such a.../to that sort of...'), and 'подлецу' is the dative of 'подлец' ('scoundrel'); the dative case marks the person at whom his emotion/hostility is directed (elliptical: 'I gnashed my teeth at such a scoundrel').
segments.p2-3-g379: 'Поросенку' is another dative noun in apposition to 'этакому подлецу', a diminutive insult 'to the little pig' (used figuratively as an insult), repeating the target for emphasis.
segments.p2-3-g380: 'Зверкову' is the surname in the dative case (final item in the series of targets), used here as the concrete person at whom the speaker's gnashing and insults are directed; the exclamation mark closes the outburst.
segments.p2-3-g431: Они is the nominative plural subject 'they'. Цинически is an adverb modifying the verb; смеялись is the past imperfective plural of смеяться ('to laugh') and here carries the sense 'laughed at / mocked'. Над governs the instrumental case and together with the instrumental phrase моим лицом marks the target of their laughter ('laughed at my face'). Word order is subject — adverb — verb — prepositional phrase, a normal S‑Adv‑V‑Obj sequence.
segments.p2-3-g432: This is a continuation of the same verbal action: над takes the instrumental, so моей мешковатой фигурой is instrumental singular feminine (моей + мешковатой + фигурой) and functions as another object of the verb 'смеялись' ('laughed at my baggy figure'). Мешковатой is an adjectival form meaning 'baggy, loose' describing фигура (body/shape).
segments.p2-3-g433: А is a contrastive conjunction ('and/yet/but'); между тем is a fixed phrase meaning 'meanwhile' or 'and yet'. Какие глупые is an exclamatory phrase ('what stupid...') with какие agreeing in gender/number with лица. У них самих is a possessive construction (у + genitive) with самих adding emphasis ('at them themselves' / 'their very own'); были is the past plural of быть; лица is the thing possessed ('faces') and appears in its plural form — the clause is inverted for emphasis (literal: 'and yet what stupid faces they themselves had!').
segments.p2-3-g434: В нашей школе is a locative phrase (в + prepositional) 'in/at our school'. Выражения лиц is the plural subject 'expressions of faces' (выражения in nominative plural, лиц genitive plural). Как-то is a particle/phrase meaning 'somehow' (как +-то forming an adverbial), особенно is an adverb 'especially'. Глупели and перерождались are past plural imperfective verbs (глупеть 'to grow stupid', перерождаться with reflexive -ся 'to be reborn / to degenerate/transform into'), agreeing with the plural subject; the reflexive suffix here marks change/passive-like or intransitive transformation.
segments.p2-3-g435: Сколько is an interrogative/ exclamatory quantitative word 'how many', which governs the genitive plural (прекрасных детей). Собой here is a postpositive emphatic/idiomatic use of себя ('oneself') meaning roughly 'in themselves' or 'in their bearing'—it intensifies the quality expressed by прекрасных. Поступало is the past neuter/impersonal form of поступать used with such quantitative constructions ('came/entered'), and к нам means 'to us' or 'to our school/institution'.
segments.p2-3-g436: Чрез несколько лет is an archaic/poetic prepositional use meaning 'after/within a few years' (чрез + accusative/amount). На них is a prepositional phrase marking the object of visual attention ('at them' / 'to look at them'). И here functions as 'even' (кажется усилительным), modifying the infinitive глядеть. The construction глядеть становилось противно is impersonal: the infinitive глядеть is the action and становилось противно means 'it became unpleasant/disgusting' to do that action — literally, 'to look became disagreeable.'
segments.p2-3-g400: Всего-навсего is a doubled colloquial adverb meaning 'only/merely' (intensifier). Лежало is the past neuter of лежать used impersonally in an existential/possessive construction ('there lay/was with me'), with у меня marking the possessor ('at me' = 'I had'). Девять рублей is the nominal expression (numeral + noun) serving as the grammatical subject; verb-before-subject word order is normal in such existential statements.
segments.p2-3-g401: Но introduces a contrast. Из них семь = 'seven of them' (из + pronoun in genitive selects part of the previously mentioned sum). Надо было + infinitive (отдать) is an impersonal past necessity construction meaning 'it was necessary to give' → 'I had to give/pay'. Завтра же places urgency ('завтра же' = 'right tomorrow'). Месячного жалованья Аполлону is the object to be given: 'a month's pay to Apollon' (месячного жалованья in genitive governed by отдать, Аполлону is dative recipient).
segments.p2-3-g402: Моему слуге is an appositive in the dative matching Аполлону: мой (possessive adjective) is declined to моему to agree with masculine dative слуге. Together: 'to Apollon, my servant'.
segments.p2-3-g403: Который introduces a relative clause modifying 'слуге' ('who'). Жил is past tense (he lived/was living). У меня here means 'at my place/with me' (location/relationship), за семь рублей indicates the rate/payment ('for seven rubles'), and на своих харчах is a prepositional phrase meaning 'on his own victuals/board' with свой reflexively referring back to the servant.
segments.p2-3-g404: Не + infinitive (выдать) expresses the negated action 'not to give'. Же is an emphatic particle adding force ('indeed/after all'), and было невозможно is an impersonal past construction 'it was impossible' — together: 'it was impossible not to give.'
segments.p2-3-g405: Судя по is a fixed idiom meaning 'judging by/according to' and governs the dative; характеру is dative ('by the character'), while Аполлона is in the genitive as the possessor ('of Apollon'). The phrase qualifies the preceding clause ('judging by Apollon's character').
segments.p2-3-g406: Но is a simple contrastive conjunction ('but'). Об is the preposition 'about/concerning' used before vowels (variant of 'о'), taking the prepositional case; этой is the demonstrative adjective in the prepositional feminine singular agreeing with каналье (prepositional of каналья).
segments.p2-3-g407: Об этой язве mirrors the previous construction with об + prepositional (язве). Моей is the possessive adjective agreeing in case/gender/number and appears after the noun (a common stylistic option) — 'about this ulcer of mine.' The choice of язва here is figurative ('a scourge/plague') rather than a literal sore.
segments.p2-3-g408: Я is the subject. Когда-нибудь + после is an adverbial phrase meaning 'sometime later' (когда-нибудь = 'sometime/ever', после = 'afterwards/later'). Поговорю is first-person singular future (perfective) 'I will speak/talk', so the whole means 'I'll speak about it sometime later.'
segments.p2-3-g381: Разумеется is an adverbial discourse particle meaning "of course" or "naturally," used here as a brief introductory comment; it stands alone with a following comma to mark the speaker's attitude.
segments.p2-3-g382: Не надо + infinitive is an impersonal modal construction meaning "there is no need to [do something]" (negative necessity); ехать is the infinitive 'to go/travel' and is not conjugated here.
segments.p2-3-g383: A repeated Разумеется, same function as the first token — a colloquial emphatic discourse marker reintroducing the next interjection.
segments.p2-3-g384: Наплевать is an impersonal colloquial verb meaning "not to care / couldn't care less" (literally 'to spit on') and here functions as a blunt exclamation; the colon that follows signals an explanatory or emphatic clause to come.
segments.p2-3-g385: Что here is an exclamatory/colloquial particle introducing a rhetorical question or challenge ("what, ..."); я is the nominative pronoun 'I' and is the subject of the following predicate.
segments.p2-3-g386: Связан is the short past passive participle / predicate adjective meaning 'tied' or 'bound' (here physical or figurative "prevented"); it is used predicatively with the subject я and set off by commas as a parenthetical description.
segments.p2-3-g387: Что ли is a colloquial tag-question: что (exclamatory/interrogative particle) + ли (enclitic question particle) combine to mean roughly '... or what?' or '... is that so?'; it softens or challenges the preceding statement.
segments.p2-3-g388: Завтра же is an adverbial phrase: завтра 'tomorrow' with the emphatic particle же (here adding immediacy/insistence). Уведомлю is the 1st person singular future (perfective) 'I will inform/notify'; Симонова is the direct object (accusative) surname 'Simonov'; по городской почте is a prepositional/instrumental phrase meaning 'by/through the city post' ('по' + dative noun).
segments.p2-3-g391: Но потому-то combines 'but' (Но) with потому-то ('for that very reason' — потому 'for that reason' plus the emphatic particle то) and the particle и before the verb adds emphasis ('indeed I...'); бесился is past-tense reflexive 'was raging/was furious' describing the speaker's strong emotional state.
segments.p2-3-g392: Что introduces a subordinate clause 'that'; наверно here functions as a strengthening adverb meaning 'surely / certainly' (contextual, not merely 'probably'); знал is the past tense 'knew.'
segments.p2-3-g393: Что + поеду is another 'that I would go' clause: поеду is the perfective future 1st person singular 'I will set off/go,' used after a past-tense reporting verb to indicate a future-in-the-past intention.
segments.p2-3-g394: Еще одно придаточное: что нарочно поеду — что (that) + нарочно (adverb 'deliberately, on purpose') modifying поеду ('I will go'), repeating and emphasizing that the act would be intentional.
segments.p2-3-g395: И чем бестактнее begins the first half of a correlative comparative construction (чем ... тем ...): и ('and') plus чем ('the more/than') introducing the comparative clause; бестактнее is the comparative form 'more tactless.'
segments.p2-3-g396: Чем неприличнее будет мне ехать continues the comparative clause: чем ('the more / than') + неприличнее ('more indecent') with the impersonal/future verb будет ('it will be') and the dative мне plus infinitive ехать giving the sense 'it will be more indecent for me to go' (dative + infinitive expresses the person affected).
segments.p2-3-g397: Тем скорее и поеду is the correlative result clause: тем ('the more / so much the') + скорее ('sooner/more quickly') and the particle и again for emphasis, followed by поеду (1st person future) — 'the more ..., the sooner I'll go.'
segments.p2-3-g398: И даже is 'and even' (adding emphasis). Препятствие положительное было is 'there was a real/positive obstacle' (положительное intensifies 'real/definite'), and the infinitive phrase не ехать functions as the content of that obstacle ('not going' or 'to not go' — i.e. the reason preventing travel); past было indicates the existence of that obstacle.
segments.p2-3-g399: Денег не было is an impersonal negative existential: денег (genitive plural of 'money') with не было means 'there was no money.' The subject (owner) is omitted here but implied from previous context.
segments.p2-3-g493: Adverb + adverb + verb + pronoun + preposition + demonstrative; the order is typical Russian: manner adverbs (Дико, насмешливо) come before the verb смотреть in the past plural смотрели, then the subject они follows (common in literary style). Смотреть на + accusative (это) = 'to look at this.'
segments.p2-3-g494: But (но) introduces a contrast; нравственно is an adverb modifying the past imperfective/iterative verb подчинялись (they submitted/obeyed). The verb is in the past plural, showing habitual or continuing submission in that period.
segments.p2-3-g495: Тем более что is a fixed connective meaning 'all the more because/especially since' and governs the clause that follows. Даже (even) is an intensifier. Обращали is past plural imperfective meaning 'directed/paid' (attention); обратить внимание на кого/что = 'to pay attention to someone/something.' По этому поводу is a set phrase 'on/about this matter.'
segments.p2-3-g496: Simple past passive-like intransitive construction: Насмешки (nominative plural) is the subject and прекратились is the short past plural reflexive form meaning 'ceased' or 'came to an end.'
segments.p2-3-g497: Contrast introduced by но 'but'; осталась is past singular feminine because неприязнь (feminine singular) is the subject and means 'remained.' Russian often uses остаться to indicate something continues to exist.
segments.p2-3-g498: И 'and' links the next clause; установились is past plural reflexive meaning 'were established' or 'settled in' and agrees with отношения (plural). Холодные here is an adjective describing the ensuing noun and functions as 'cold' in the social/attitudinal sense (aloof).
segments.p2-3-g499: Натянутые (past-participle-like adjective) + отношения (nominative plural) is the noun phrase 'strained relations.' In Russian adjectives precede nouns and agree in number and case.
segments.p2-3-g500: Под конец is an adverbial phrase meaning 'towards the end' or 'finally.' Я сам uses сам for emphatic 'I myself.' Не выдержал is past masculine singular negative of выдержать meaning 'couldn't endure' or 'couldn't stand it.' The colon marks an explanation that follows.
segments.p2-3-g501: С летами is an instrumental plural time-expression meaning 'with the years' or 'as years passed.' Развивалась is imperfective past (feminine singular agreeing with потребность) meaning 'developed' or 'was developing.' Потребность в людях uses потребность + в + prepositional plural to indicate 'a need for people.'
segments.p2-3-g502: Short fragment: в друзьях is prepositional plural meaning 'in friends' but functions idiomatically as 'for friends' (a need for friends). The preposition-в + locative plural marks the object of потребность.
segments.p2-3-g503: Пробовал было is a common past construction where было after a past verb gives an attenuated or momentary sense 'had tried (for a while)' or 'was about to try' — the particle было softens/limits the action. Начать + infinitive (сближаться) = 'to begin to get closer.' С + иными = 'with others.' Word order is subject (Я) + verb phrase + infinitive + complement.
segments.p2-3-g504: Но introduces contrast; всегда (always) modifies the whole clause. Это сближение is a demonstrative noun phrase 'this rapprochement.' Выходило is imperfective past (выходить) used idiomatically 'turned out' or 'proved to be'; it describes the result or manner. Неестественно (adverb) modifies выходило. Так само собой is a set of adverbial particles meaning 'thus, by itself' (само собой = 'of itself'); оканчивалось is imperfective past reflexive 'ended' or 'came to an end.'
segments.p2-3-g423: The clause has subject + verb + object order: Товарищи (subject) встретили (past tense, perfective) меня (direct object). The manner is expressed by the instrumental plural насмешками with coordinating adjectives злобными и безжалостными agreeing in case, number, and gender; за то here ('за' + demonstrative то) introduces the reason clause that follows (за то, что ...).
segments.p2-3-g424: Что introduces a subordinate explanatory clause. The phrase ни на кого из них is a negative indefinite: ни ... не (with ни before на кого) = 'not anyone/none', where на кого is the prepositional construction required by похож (похож на = 'to resemble'). Из них ('of them') narrows the reference. Не был похож is the past tense of быть + short-form adjective похож (predicate 'was not like/resembled'), so the whole means 'that I resembled none of them.'
segments.p2-3-g425: Не мог + infinitive переносить expresses past inability: мог is the past tense of мочь (ability), переносить is the imperfective infinitive 'to tolerate/endure'. Насмешек is genitive plural (partitive/indefinite object) after the negated verb, so 'could not tolerate (any) mockery.'
segments.p2-3-g426: Again не мог + infinitive (уживаться) = 'could not manage to/get along'. Так дешево is an adverbial phrase modifying уживаться: дешево here is figurative 'in a cheap/mean way' and так strengthens it ('so/that way'). Уживаться is a reflexive verb meaning 'to live/get along with others.'
segments.p2-3-g427: Как introduces a comparison clause ('as/like'), not a temporal clause. Они уживались is past imperfective 'they got along', and друг с другом is an idiomatic reciprocal expression 'with one another' (другом is instrumental in the set phrase друг с другом).
segments.p2-3-g428: Возненавидел is a perfective past-tense verb meaning 'came to hate' / 'hated intensely' and takes их as its direct object. Тотчас ('immediately') modifies the verb. Заключился (reflexive past) here means 'shut oneself up' or 'enclosed oneself'; от всех ('from everyone', with всех in genitive) marks separation, and в + adjective (пугливую) begins the phrase 'into a timid...' where the noun follows in the next segment. Note the shift from one completed emotional verb to a reflexive state verb.
segments.p2-3-g429: The adjectives уязвленную и непомерную both are accusative feminine singular to agree with the noun гордость (also accusative) governed by в + accusative ('into ... pride'). Уязвленную is a past-participial adjective 'offended/wounded', непомерную means 'excessive/undue'; together they describe the type of pride he enclosed himself in.
segments.p2-3-g430: Грубость их is a nominative subject phrase ('rudeness of them' = 'their rudeness'); placing the possessive pronoun after the noun adds emphasis/colloquiality. Меня is the accusative object of the verb возмутила (feminine past tense of возмутить), which means 'outraged/disturbed' and is used here in the perfective past to indicate a completed emotional reaction.
segments.p2-3-g409: Впрочем is a sentence‑initial discourse particle meaning 'anyway/however' used to pivot or soften what follows; it doesn't change the clause grammar but signals a shift or reservation.
segments.p2-3-g410: Я (I) is the subject pronoun; ведь is a pragmatic particle ('after all' / 'you know') adding emphasis to the assertion; знал is the past tense (masculine singular) of знать and here simply reports past knowledge ('I knew'). The three together mean 'I, after all, knew'.
segments.p2-3-g411: Что introduces a subordinate clause ('that'). Все-таки is an adverbial phrase (всё + таки) meaning 'still' / 'after all' / 'nevertheless' modifying the following verb; не is the negation; выдам is 1st person singular future of выдать — here best read as 'give oneself away / reveal (something)' rather than the literal 'issue'. Together: 'that I still wouldn't give myself away.'
segments.p2-3-g412: А is a coordinating particle/conjunction here contrasting the two parts ('but/and'); непременно is an adverb 'certainly/without fail'; поеду is 1st person singular future of поехать ('set off / go away'). The clause asserts the speaker will nevertheless depart: 'but I will certainly go.'
segments.p2-3-g413: В эту ночь is a temporal accusative expression 'that/this night' (в + accusative) indicating when; снились is the past plural of сниться agreeing with the plural noun 'сны' and meaning 'appeared in dreams / were dreamed'; мне is the dative experiencer 'to me'; безобразнейшие is the superlative adjective 'most hideous'; сны is the plural noun 'dreams' and functions as the grammatical subject of снились. Literal order corresponds to 'On this night most hideous dreams appeared to me.'
segments.p2-3-g414: Не + мудрено is an impersonal phrase meaning 'no wonder' or 'it's not surprising.' Here не negates the predicative мудрено (an impersonal adjective/short form), producing a comment on the following sentence.
segments.p2-3-g415: Весь вечер is a temporal phrase 'the whole evening'; давили is past plural of давить because the logical subject (воспоминания) is plural — it means 'pressed' or 'oppressed'; меня is the direct object 'me'; воспоминания is the plural subject 'memories'; о + каторжных годах моей школьной жизни is a prepositional phrase specifying the content of those memories ('about the penal years of my school life'). The word order places the time phrase first and leaves the plural subject after the verb for emphasis.
segments.p2-3-g416: И 'and' links to the next clause; я не мог is the past-tense modal 'I could not' (мочь + past); от них is a prepositional phrase 'from them' or 'of them' marking the source; отвязаться is the perfective infinitive 'to free oneself / to shake off.' The construction не мог + infinitive expresses inability in the past: 'I couldn't shake them off.'
segments.p2-3-g417: Меня сунули is a colloquial, transitive past construction literally 'they shoved/put me' with the patient in accusative; в эту школу marks the destination ('into this school'); мои дальние родственники following the verb name the agents ('my distant relatives'). The verb is past plural (they) and the agent phrase is postverbal.
segments.p2-3-g418: От которых ... зависел begins a relative clause 'from whom I depended' (от takes which/whom in the appropriate case); и introduces the next coordinated relative 'and о которых ... не имел никакого понятия' = 'and about whom since then I had no notion/understanding.' С тех пор is the fixed time expression 'since then'; никакого поняти́я is the negated indefinite object 'no notion/understanding.' The clause uses standard prepositional/relative patterns (от которых / о которых).
segments.p2-3-g419: The dash continues the narrative and the repeated verb сунули introduces a new accusative attribute: 'they put (slipped) in the orphaned one.' Сиротливого is an adjectival/epithet in the accusative describing the child ('forlorn, orphan‑like').
segments.p2-3-g420: Уже is 'already'; забитого is a past participle in accusative singular agreeing with the child and here means 'crushed / beaten down' (from забить) by their попреками — instrumental plural 'by their reproaches.' The participial phrase modifies the orphaned child.
segments.p2-3-g421: Уже again 'already'; задумывающегося is a present active participle (accusative singular) meaning 'beginning to ponder / becoming pensive,' continuing the string of participial descriptions of the child.
segments.p2-3-g422: Молчаливого is another adjectival descriptor 'silent'; и connects it to the next descriptive phrase; дико is an adverbial intensifier 'wildly/terribly'; на все озиравшегося is a participial phrase ('looking around at everything') with на + accusative 'на все' ('at everything') and the reflexive participle озиравшегося agreeing with the child. Together they paint the child's demeanor: silent and wildly looking around at everything.
segments.p2-3-g482: "Но" is the coordinating conjunction 'but'; the clause has the simple S-V-O order: "я" (subject) + "уже не желал" (past tense with уже + negative = 'no longer desired') + "их любви" (genitive object because желать governs genitive). The phrase reads naturally as 'I no longer desired their love.'
segments.p2-3-g483: "напротив" is an adverbial connective meaning 'on the contrary' and here functions as a sentence adverb that reverses the expectation set by the previous clause.
segments.p2-3-g484: The clause again has subject + adverb + verb + genitive object: "я" + "постоянно" (adverb 'constantly') + "жаждал" (past imperfective 'yearned/longed for') + "их унижения" (genitive, object of жаждать). Жаждать takes genitive and expresses a strong, continuing desire.
segments.p2-3-g485: "Чтоб" is the colloquial contraction of "чтобы" meaning 'in order to'; it introduces a purpose infinitive clause. "Избавить" is the perfective infinitive 'to free/spare', "себя" is the reflexive accusative ('myself'), and "от их насмешек" is a prepositional phrase with "от" + genitive indicating the source ('from their mockery').
segments.p2-3-g486: Main clause: "я" (subject) + "нарочно" (adverb 'deliberately') + "начал" + infinitive "учиться" (начал + infinitive = 'began to study'). The phrase "как можно лучше" is an idiom meaning 'as well/best as possible'. "И пробился в число самых первых" is coordinated by "и": "пробился" (past of пробиться 'to make one's way/manage to get') + "в" + "число самых первых" ('into the number/ranks of the very first'), i.e. 'got into the top ranks.'
segments.p2-3-g487: "Это" is the neuter demonstrative pronoun 'this/it' and is the grammatical subject; "им" is the dative 'to them'; "внушило" is the perfective past neuter form of внушить ('instilled, suggested'), neuter to agree with "это". The construction means 'this impressed/was suggested to them.'
segments.p2-3-g488: "К тому же" is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'moreover' or 'besides'; grammatically it acts as a discourse connector. "Все они" ('all of them') is the topicalized subject; "начали" (past plural) + infinitive "понимать" gives 'began to understand', and "помаленьку" modifies the verb as 'little by little.'
segments.p2-3-g489: "Что" introduces the content clause 'that I ...'; "я уже читал" uses past tense with "уже" to indicate the action had taken place already ('I had already read' or 'I had read already'); "такие книги" is the direct object in the plural (accusative) meaning 'such books.'
segments.p2-3-g490: "Которых" is the relative pronoun in genitive plural agreeing with книги and heads the relative clause; word order "которых они не могли читать" is standard: subject "они" + negated past ability "не могли" + infinitive "читать" = 'which they could not read.'
segments.p2-3-g491: Within the same content clause (subject still 'я' from earlier), "и понимал" coordinates another past-tense verb 'and I understood' followed by object "такие вещи" ('such things'). The parenthetical participial phrase "(не входившие в состав нашего специального курса)" is a past-participle clause (входившие) modifying "вещи": it literally says these things 'not included in the composition of our special course' (i.e. outside the official curriculum).
segments.p2-3-g492: "О которых" is the preposition + relative pronoun ('about which'); "они" is the subject; the particle "и" here functions as an intensifier, 'not even' ("и не слыхивали" = 'had not even heard of'); "слыхивали" is past imperfective meaning 'to have heard (of)'.
segments.p2-3-g457: "Напротив" is a discourse adverb meaning "on the contrary," used here to signal opposition to the previous statement; it stands alone and sets the contrastive tone for the whole sentence.
segments.p2-3-g458: The phrase "самую очевидную" is an intensified adjective in the feminine accusative singular (самую = superlative/intensifier + accusative agreement with the feminine noun that follows). It modifies the noun phrase that follows and is fronted for emphasis, separated by a comma.
segments.p2-3-g459: "Режущую глаза действительность" is a noun phrase in the accusative (действительность, feminine) with a participial modifier (режущую глаза = "that cuts the eyes" → "eye‑piercing/harsh to the eye"). "Они принимали" is past‑tense, imperfective, plural: "they accepted/took (it)"; the adverbial string "фантастически глупо" modifies that verb (degree + manner). The coordinated clause "и уже тогда привыкли поклоняться одному успеху" uses "уже тогда" ("even then/at that time") with "привыкли" (past plural, "had grown accustomed") plus an infinitive "поклоняться"; note "успеху" is dative because "поклоняться" governs the dative ("to worship/idolize success").
segments.p2-3-g460: "Все" is the neuter pronoun meaning "everything" (subject of the following relative clause). It is the item being commented on by the relative clause "что было..." that follows.
segments.p2-3-g461: "что было справедливо" is a relative clause: "что" = "which/that," "было" = past neuter singular copula, and "справедливо" is a predicative meaning "just/fair". Together: "that was just."
segments.p2-3-g462: "но унижено и забито" continues the relative clause with two short participial/adjectival predicates (neuter to agree with "всё"): "унижено" (humiliated) and "забито" (literally "driven/hammered in" — here figuratively "beaten down/pressed down"). The coordinating "но" marks contrast ("but").
segments.p2-3-g463: "над тем" is the preposition "над" + demonstrative/adverbial "тем" (instrumental/that-thing), meaning "over/at that" ("to laugh at something" uses над + instrumental). "Они" is the explicit subject, and "жестокосердно и позорно" are adverbial modifiers (two adverbs in coordination) describing the manner of "смеялись" (past plural imperfective, "they laughed/mocked"). The verb here implies mocking or derisive laughter directed at the items previously described.
segments.p2-3-g464: Word order is inverted for emphasis: "Чин" (rank) is fronted as the theme, then the past plural verb "почитали" (they revered/considered) appears with the idiomatic construction "почитать за" meaning "to regard something as something." "За ум" is the complement meaning "as intelligence" ("ум" = intellect).
segments.p2-3-g465: "В шестнадцать лет" is a temporal prepositional phrase indicating age/time ("at sixteen"); note numerals in Russian use the genitive plural after the numeral for "лет." "Уже" (already) modifies the past plural verb "толковали" (they were discussing/expounding). The verb takes the preposition "о" (about) and the prepositional plural "теплых местечках" ("теплых" agrees in number/case with "местечках"); the idiom "теплое местечко" means a comfortable or cushy little posting.
segments.p2-3-g466: Конечно is an adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally' and functions parenthetically at the start of the sentence to signal agreement or emphasis; it is separated by a comma in normal written style.
segments.p2-3-g467: This is an impersonal past construction: много было = 'there was much' or 'much of it was'. Тут ('here' / 'in this situation') localizes the statement. От governs the genitive глупости, indicating cause ('because of' or 'from'). Note the topical fronting 'много тут было' (placing 'много' first for emphasis).
segments.p2-3-g468: Another causal prepositional phrase: от + genitive (дурного примера) = 'from/because of the bad example'. Дурного is a genitive singular adjective modifying примера because of the preposition.
segments.p2-3-g469: This participial phrase modifies пример(a). Окружавшего is the past active participle in the genitive singular (agreeing with примера), with беспрерывно as its adverbial modifier ('had been continuously surrounding'). Их is the genitive plural possessive 'their' modifying детство и отрочество ('childhood and adolescence').
segments.p2-3-g437: Еще в шестнадцать лет is a temporal adverbial phrase meaning 'as early as/at sixteen'. «я угрюмо на них дивился» is a past-tense clause: subject я, adverb угрюмо (sullenly) modifies the verb дивился (past, masculine singular). дивиться takes на + accusative (на них = 'at them/at those people').
segments.p2-3-g438: меня is the accusative object ('me'); уж и тогда is an adverbial cluster meaning 'already, even then' (и here intensifies уж). изумляли is past plural of изумлять 'to astonish' and is plural because it agrees with the following coordinated nouns (мелочь, глупость, игр, разговоров). мелочь их мышления is the first coordinated noun phrase: мелочь (= pettiness) + genitive их мышления 'of their thinking'.
segments.p2-3-g439: глупость is a nominative noun in the coordinated list meaning 'stupidity/foolishness'; их занятий is genitive plural 'of their occupations/activities' and completes the noun phrase 'the stupidity of their pursuits'.
segments.p2-3-g440: игр is the genitive plural of игра, here continuing the list of things that astonished the narrator: 'their games' (the genitive follows the pattern of a coordinated list after изумляли).
segments.p2-3-g441: разговоров is genitive plural of разговор 'conversations/talks', the final item in the coordinated list of what astonished him.
segments.p2-3-g442: Они is the plural subject 'they'. таких необходимых вещей is a genitive plural phrase 'such necessary things/matters' (таких + необходимых agreeing), and не понимали is past plural negative 'did not understand'.
segments.p2-3-g443: такими внушающими is an adjectival/participle phrase in the instrumental plural (matching the noun предметами that follows): такими 'such' + внушающими (present participle used adjectivally) meaning 'inspiring/instilling' — 'by such inspiring ...'.
segments.p2-3-g444: поражающими предметами is also instrumental plural: поражающими (present participle 'astonishing/striking') modifying предметами ('subjects/objects'). не интересовались is past plural negative of интересоваться 'were not interested (in)'.
segments.p2-3-g445: что introduces the result clause ('so that/which'), поневоле is an adverb 'unavoidably/inevitably', я стал считать is a periphrastic past meaning 'I began to consider' (стал + infinitive считать), их ниже себя is the object phrase 'them lower than myself' (ниже = comparative 'lower').
segments.p2-3-g446: Не оскорбленное тщеславие is a noun phrase where оскорбленное is a past passive participle modifying тщеславие: 'not offended/wounded vanity'. подбивало (past, neuter singular) agrees with тщеславие (neuter) and подбивать сюда means 'to urge/drive on'; меня is the accusative object, к тому is a dative phrase 'to that/to such a point'.
segments.p2-3-g447: и is the simple coordinating conjunction 'and' linking clauses.
segments.p2-3-g448: ради бога is a fixed exclamation 'for God's sake' (ради = 'for the sake of' + бога in genitive).
segments.p2-3-g449: не вылезайте ко мне с ... возражениями is an idiomatic imperative 'don't come at me with ... objections' (вылезайте here colloquial imperative of вылезать). с introduces an instrumental phrase: приевшимися до тошноты казенными возражениями — приевшимися (past participle, instrumental pl.) 'grown tiresome/cloying', до тошноты 'to the point of nausea', казенными 'bureaucratic/official' modifies возражениями 'objections'.
segments.p2-3-g450: Opening the quoted objection: что is the conjunction 'that', я is the subject 'I', только is an adverb 'only/merely', мечтал is past imperfective 'dreamed/was dreaming' (implies daydreaming rather than fulfilled plan).
segments.p2-3-g451: а introduces a contrast 'while/but', они is 'they', уж и тогда (adverbial cluster) = 'already even then' (уж = already, и intensifies), действительную жизнь is the accusative object 'real/actual life' and понимали is past plural 'understood'.
segments.p2-3-g452: Ничего fronted for emphasis is 'nothing' (accusative), они is the subject 'they', не понимали is past plural negative 'did not understand'. The fronting of ничего gives strong negative emphasis.
segments.p2-3-g453: никакой действительной жизни is a negative noun phrase in genitive ('no/none of any real life' or more naturally 'no real life') clarifying what they didn't understand; действительной modifies жизнь in the genitive.
segments.p2-3-g454: и is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking the clauses.
segments.p2-3-g455: клянусь is first-person present 'I swear' (used parenthetically to strengthen the statement).
segments.p2-3-g456: это-то is a demonstrative + emphasizing particle ('this very thing'); the particle то adds emphasis. и here functions as an emphatic particle ('indeed/precisely' as part of the phrase 'это-то и возмущало'), возмущало is past neuter singular agreeing with это-то 'outraged/angered', меня is the object 'me', в них 'in them', наиболее 'most/above all'.
segments.p2-3-g470: Развратны is a short-form adjective in predicate position (plural) meaning 'depraved/dissolute'; они is the nominative plural subject; были is the past-tense copula (plural) completing the verbal phrase; до + noun in genitive (уродливости) here means 'to the point of' or 'to an extent of' that noun.
segments.p2-3-g471: Разумеется is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally' and sets a tone of concession or acknowledgment for the following clause.
segments.p2-3-g472: и is a simple coordinating conjunction 'and/also'; тут is an adverb here meaning 'in this situation/among them'; было is the impersonal/past existential 'there was'; больше + noun in genitive (внешности) expresses 'more of appearance' (i.e. more appearance than substance).
segments.p2-3-g473: больше quantifies the following noun phrase (напускной циничности) — 'more (of) affected cynicism'; напускной is an adjective meaning 'put-on/affected' and циничности is in the genitive as the quantified noun.
segments.p2-3-g474: Разумеется repeated as a parenthetical 'of course', echoing the previous concession.
segments.p2-3-g475: The phrase has a compound subject 'юность и некоторая свежесть' (nominative) and the verb мелькали (past plural) means 'flashed by / appeared briefly'; и в них places that fleeting quality 'also/in them'; даже + из-за indicates emphasis 'even because of / on account of' and разврата is in the genitive after из-за ('because of debauchery').
segments.p2-3-g476: но is the adversative 'but'; непривлекательна is a short-form predicate adjective (feminine singular) placed before the copula была for effect — it agrees with свежесть (fem. sing.); в них 'in them' indicates location/possession; даже и is an emphatic combination 'even'; the second clause и проявлялась uses the reflexive/impersonal verb 'проявлялась' 'manifested itself' and in каком-то ерничестве the prepositional phrase means 'in some sort of sneering/mockery' (каком-то = 'some kind of').
segments.p2-3-g477: Я is the subject 'I'; ненавидел is past tense imperfective 'hated' (habitual/ongoing in the past); их is the accusative object 'them'; ужасно is an adverb intensifier here meaning 'extremely/terribly'.
segments.p2-3-g478: хотя is the subordinating conjunction 'although/though', introducing a contrast or qualification to the previous statement.
segments.p2-3-g479: пожалуй is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'one might say', softening or qualifying the assertion that follows.
segments.p2-3-g480: был is the past copula 'was'; их here functions as the genitive/comparative complement meaning 'than them' (хуже их = 'worse than them'); же is an emphatic particle strengthening the contrast ('even / precisely'); хуже is the comparative 'worse' (comparative form of плохой) completing the comparison.
segments.p2-3-g481: Они is the subject 'they'; мне is an indirect-object/dative 'to me'; тем же ('by the same / with the same') is an idiomatic instrumental phrase meaning 'in the same way' or 'in kind' and is the object of платили (past plural 'paid/repaid'); и links the second clause; не скрывали is 'did not hide' (past, plural); своего is the reflexive possessive referring back to 'они' ('their own'); ко мне is a prepositional/directional phrase 'toward me' (ко is a variant of к before certain pronouns); омерзения is the noun 'disgust/loathing' in the genitive as the object of скрывали.
segments.p2-3-g534: 'С непривычки' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'from lack of habit / out of unfamiliarity'. The preposition 'с' here means 'from' and governs the noun in the genitive, and 'непривычки' is a noun used in that genitive sense to indicate cause.
segments.p2-3-g535: 'что ли' is a colloquial particle combination expressing uncertainty or tentativeness (roughly 'perhaps / maybe / I wonder'). 'что' here functions as a particle rather than the relative 'that/what', and 'ли' is the enclitic particle that marks the uncertainty.
segments.p2-3-g536: ‘но мне всю жизнь’ literally 'but to me my whole life': 'но' = 'but', 'мне' is the dative experiencer 'to me' and is fronted for emphasis, and 'всю жизнь' = 'all my life' (accusative with the preposition omitted). Together this sets the time/span and viewpoint for the following predicate.
segments.p2-3-g537: 'при всяком внешнем' is a prepositional phrase with 'при' meaning 'on/at the occasion of' and taking the prepositional case; 'всяком' = 'every/any', 'внешнем' = 'outward/external'. It begins a clause specifying the circumstances ('on every outward ...').
segments.p2-3-g538: 'хотя бы мельчайшем событии' completes the circumstantial phrase: 'хотя бы' here means 'even' or 'at least' (softening/emphatic), with the particle 'бы' as a modal/optative particle; 'мельчайшем событии' is 'the tiniest event' in the same case governed by 'при'.
segments.p2-3-g539: 'все казалось' is the main impersonal-like phrase: 'всё' = 'everything/it all', and 'казалось' is the past tense of 'казаться' ('seemed') used with an experiencer (мне earlier). Grammatically the neuter singular verb agrees with 'всё'.
segments.p2-3-g540: This subordinate clause begins with 'что' = 'that' (introducing what seemed), then the particles 'вот сейчас и' emphasize immediacy ('right now / just now / exactly now' with 'и' giving an 'indeed/even' force). 'наступит' is future, third person singular of the perfective verb 'наступить' meaning 'will arrive / will come about'. 'какой-нибудь' is the indefinite pronoun 'some/any' (here 'some kind of'), 'радикальный перелом' is a figurative 'radical turning point' (not a literal fracture), and 'в моей жизни' = 'in my life' (prepositional phrase marking sphere/location).
segments.p2-3-g541: Я is the nominative first-person singular pronoun 'I', the grammatical subject of the sentence; the following comma marks a brief pause before the parenthetical vпрочем.
segments.p2-3-g542: впрочем is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'however' or 'indeed/anyway' used to signal a contrast or qualification; it is commonly set off by commas.
segments.p2-3-g543: Отправился is the past tense, reflexive form meaning 'set off' or 'went' (here: to duty). В должность is a fixed collocation meaning 'to the post/office' (в + accusative/directional sense 'into/toward'). По-обыкновенному is an adverbial phrase 'as usual' (по + adjective in the adverbial sense), describing the manner.
segments.p2-3-g544: Но introduces a contrast ('but'). Улизнул is a colloquial past verb 'slipped away' or 'sneaked off'. Домой is a directional adverb meaning 'home' (toward home). Двумя часами раньше (expressed here as 'двумя часами раньше') is a time-difference expression using the instrumental (часами) with a numeral to mean 'by two hours earlier' ('two hours earlier').
segments.p2-3-g545: Чтоб is a colloquial form of чтобы used here to indicate purpose ('in order that' / 'so as to'); приготовиться is the perfective infinitive 'to prepare oneself' or 'to get ready', expressing the purpose of slipping home early.
segments.p2-3-g546: Главное is a neuter adjective used as a nominalized noun in the nominative, meaning "the main thing" or "the most important point" and functions as the topic/heading of the sentence.
segments.p2-3-g547: Думал я is a past-tense, first-person statement in the imperfective (I thought/I was thinking); the word order (verb before pronoun) gives it a parenthetical, reflective tone: "I thought (to myself)."
segments.p2-3-g548: Надо приехать combines the impersonal modal надо + infinitive приехать to express necessity/obligation ("one must arrive" / "I must arrive"). Не первым uses the instrumental form первого (первым) with negation to indicate position/sequence ("not as the first/first one"), so the whole phrase means "must not arrive first."
segments.p2-3-g549: А то is a fixed colloquial connective meaning "otherwise" or "or else," and подумают is the 3rd-person plural future of подумать (they will think/assume), introducing the feared consequence.
segments.p2-3-g550: Что introduces the content clause ("that ..."). Уж is an intensifying particle here ("really/quite/at all") and очень further intensifies. Обрадовался is the perfective past masculine singular of обрадоваться meaning "became glad / was pleased," so the subordinate clause means "that I had been far too pleased."
segments.p2-3-g551: Но is a contrasting conjunction ("but"). Таких главных вещей is a genitive plural noun phrase (таких + главных + вещей) because the quantifier follows; it means "of such important things." Были тысячи is an existential construction placing the numeral thousands in the nominative and the quantified noun in the genitive: "there were thousands (of ...)."
segments.p2-3-g552: И все они волновали меня до бессилия: и = "and," все они = "all of them/they all," волновали is past plural of волновать ("disturbed/worried/agitated") agreeing with они, and до + genitive бессилия expresses extent: "to the point of powerlessness/helplessness."
segments.p2-3-g520: Первым делом моим is an instrumental-phrase idiom meaning “the first thing of mine” (i.e., the first thing I did); моим follows делом as a possessive in the instrumental. По выходе из школы is a temporal phrase with по + noun in the prepositional meaning “upon/at the time of leaving school.” Было + infinitive (было оставить) is an impersonal past construction stating what that first thing amounted to; оставить takes the accusative object ту специальную службу.
segments.p2-3-g521: К которой introduces a relative clause modifying службу; к + relative pronoun is used because предназначаться here governs the preposition к (to/toward). Я предназначался is past tense, reflexive/passive-like use of предназначаться meaning “I was intended/destined (for it).”
segments.p2-3-g522: Чтобы introduces purpose/intent: “in order to.” Все нити is the accusative plural direct object (figurative “all ties/threads”), and порвать is a perfective infinitive expressing a decisive action (“to tear/break off”). This infinitive is part of a series of coordinated aims.
segments.p2-3-g523: This is a coordination of two more perfective infinitives describing the same purpose: проклясть прошлое (“to curse/condemn the past”) and прахом его посыпать. Прахом is instrumental (lit. “with dust/ashes”) and его is the pronoun referring back to прошлое; посыпать here means “to strew/cover (with dust),” a figurative image of humiliation or obliteration.
segments.p2-3-g526: Черт знает зачем is an idiomatic, colloquial exclamation meaning “the devil knows why” (used as a rhetorical ‘I don’t know why’). После того is a temporal phrase “after that” (того = genitive demonstrative). Потащился is a past reflexive/perfective verb meaning to drag or trudge oneself (implying reluctance), and к этому Симонову is the dative phrase “to that Simonov” (э́тому + surname in dative).
segments.p2-3-g529: Утром is a temporal adverbial (“in the morning”). Я рано gives subject + adverb “I (got up) early.” Схватился с постели uses схватиться in the past to mean one suddenly sat up or grabbed oneself up from bed; с with постели is “from the bed” (с = from here).
segments.p2-3-g530: Вскочил is past tense “jumped up/leapt.” С волнением uses с + instrumental to indicate manner: “with excitement/anxiety.” The short clause gives the manner of the sudden movement.
segments.p2-3-g531: Точно here functions adverbially as “as if.” Все это = “all this.” Сейчас же is an emphatic time phrase meaning “right now/at this very moment” (же intensifies immediacy). И начнет совершаться is a future-oriented construction: начнет (3rd sing. future) + совершенствоваться (reflexive infinitive) = “will begin to take place,” with и adding emphasis/continuation.
segments.p2-3-g532: Но is a simple adversative conjunction “but.” Я верил is imperfective past “I believed” (indicates an ongoing conviction at that time), standing alone as the matrix clause to the following subordinate clause.
segments.p2-3-g533: Что introduces the subordinate content clause “that ….” Наступает (present) and наступит (future, perfective) are used together rhetorically: the present tense gives immediacy (“is coming/coming on”), while the perfective future (наступит) asserts certainty—reinforced by непременно (“certainly/without fail”). Сегодня же places the event firmly “today” with же adding emphasis. Какой-то is an indefinite phrase (“some kind of”), радикальный перелом is the noun phrase “radical turning point,” and в моей жизни is the locative phrase “in my life.”
segments.p2-3-g505: Был is the past tense of быть used existentially ('there was' / 'I had'); у меня is a possessive construction literally 'at me' meaning 'I had'. Раз как-то is an adverbial phrase: раз = 'once' and как-то makes it 'somehow/once in a way'. И here has an emphatic 'even/also' sense before друг, which is the nominative noun 'friend' as the thing that existed/had.
segments.p2-3-g506: Но 'but'; я уже был is past tense 'I was already'. Деспот в душе is a predicative noun phrase 'a despot in (my) soul/at heart' — душe here carries the meaning 'heart/emotional core' and follows the locative preposition в.
segments.p2-3-g507: я хотел is past 'I wanted' (imperfective, stating desire); неограниченно is an adverb 'without limits'; властвовать над + noun expresses 'to rule/hold power over' and takes the preposition над; его душой uses душой in the instrumental (as required after властвовать над) meaning 'over his soul'.
segments.p2-3-g508: я хотел вселить — 'I wished to instill': вселить (perfective) + в него (into him, accusative) indicates planting a feeling within him. Презрение к takes the noun 'contempt' with the preposition к ('toward'); окружавшей его среде is a participial phrase 'the environment that surrounded him' where окружавшей is a present participle agreeing in case/gender with среде (dative) and его is the object of окружавшей.
segments.p2-3-g509: я потребовал от него — 'I demanded of him' (потребовать is perfective 'to demand'); высокиймерного и окончательного разрыва is the direct object in genitive (потребовать + genitive noun) meaning 'an arrogant and final rupture/break'; с этой средой uses с + instrumental to mean 'a break with this milieu'.
segments.p2-3-g510: Я испугал его — past perfective 'I frightened him'; моей страстной дружбой uses the instrumental (дружбой) to mark the means or cause ('with my passionate friendship'), and the adjectives agree in gender/case with дружбой.
segments.p2-3-g511: я доводил его до слез — доводил (imperfective past) indicates repeatedly or habitually 'I drove/led him' and до + genitive (слез) marks the end point 'to tears'.
segments.p2-3-g512: до судорог — continuation of the previous phrase: до + genitive (судорог) 'up to convulsions/spasms'; elliptical parallelism to 'до слез'.
segments.p2-3-g513: он был наивная и отдающаяся душа — 'he was a naive and self-surrendering soul'. Note the syntactic pattern: он (masculine pronoun) is the subject, but the predicate is душа (feminine) with adjectives agreeing with душа (наивная, отдающаяся); this is normal when the predicative noun expresses the person's character.
segments.p2-3-g514: но когда он отдался мне весь — 'but when he gave himself to me entirely': когда 'when'; отдаться + dative (мне) 'to give oneself up/devote oneself to someone'; весь 'whole/entire' modifies the act of surrender.
segments.p2-3-g515: я тотчас же возненавидел его и оттолкнул от себя — 'I immediately came to hate him and pushed him away from me'. Тотчас же is an emphatic temporal phrase 'right away'; возненавидел is perfective 'came to hate'; оттолкнул (perf.) 'pushed away', от себя 'from myself'.
segments.p2-3-g516: точно он и нужен был мне только для одержания над ним победы — 'as if he had been needed by me only in order to gain victory over him': точно here ≈ 'as if'; the clause uses the predicative construction 'он нужен мне' (he was needed by me) with был indicating past, and только для + noun phrase gives the sole purpose. Одержания (gerundial noun) is 'obtaining/winning' and над ним победы 'victory over him' places the modifier над ним before победы (stylistic word order).
segments.p2-3-g517: для одного его подчинения — 'for the one/sole thing: his submission'. Одного is used substantively to mean 'the single/sole' (emphasizing exclusivity), and его подчинения is a genitive noun phrase 'his submission/subjugation' giving the purpose.
segments.p2-3-g518: Но всех я не мог победить — 'But I could not defeat all of them'. Всех (accusative plural) placed before the subject я gives emphasis; не мог победить is past inability ('could not defeat').
segments.p2-3-g519: мой друг был тоже ни на одного из них не похож и составлял самое редкое исключение — 'my friend was also unlike any one of them and made up the rarest exception.' Ни на одного из них is an idiomatic negative phrase 'not like a single one of them' (ни ... не похож = 'not similar to any'), and составлял + noun means 'constituted/amounted to' with самое редкое исключение being a superlative noun phrase 'the very rare exception.'
segments.p2-3-g565: A loanword (вицмундир) in the nominative used as the sentence's topic/subject; here it names the ceremonial/officer's coat. It's simply presented as the item under discussion, set off by a comma.
segments.p2-3-g566: Пожалуй is a parenthetical modal adverb expressing tentativeness ('perhaps'/'probably') that qualifies the preceding noun phrase; it does not change syntax but softens the assertion.
segments.p2-3-g567: Был is the past-tense auxiliary of быть and исправен is the short-form adjective meaning 'in working/order' or 'in decent condition'; together they form a predicative past statement 'was in order'. The short-form adjective (исправен) is used instead of the full form (исправный) to make a stative predicate.
segments.p2-3-g568: This clause contains an adversative particle (но) + negation (не) with a locative phrase (в вицмундире — prepositional case) and an emphatic particle (же), followed by an impersonal past-form было + infinitive construction (ехать обедать). The construction не ... было ехать обедать means 'it wasn't (appropriate/possible/proper) to go to dinner in a vitsmundir' — literally 'it was not (to) go to dine in a vitsmundir', with the locative indicating 'in/while wearing the vitsmundir.'
segments.p2-3-g569: А главное is a connective phrase meaning 'and (or) most importantly' or 'above all'; А is the coordinating particle ('and/while') here functioning like 'and', and главное (neuter) functions as a nominalized adjective meaning 'the main thing' / 'above all'.
segments.p2-3-g570: На + plural prepositional панталонах locates the stain on the pantaloons. На here is the locative preposition 'on' and панталонах is the prepositional plural form of 'pantaloons', marking place.
segments.p2-3-g571: На самой коленке is an emphatic locative phrase: самой (feminine short/demonstrative) means 'the very' or 'right' (as in 'right on the knee'), agreeing with коленке (prepositional of коленка). Было introduces an existential clause ('there was') and is followed by the delayed subject огромное желтое пятно. Note the inversion: location + было + subject = 'on the knee there was a huge yellow stain.'
segments.p2-3-g572: Я предчувствовал is a simple past statement: я (I) + предчувствовал (past tense of предчувствовать 'to sense beforehand/anticipate'), expressing that the narrator felt or foresaw something. The verb is imperfective/past and describes an internal anticipation.
segments.p2-3-g573: This is a subordinate clause introduced by что ('that'). Одно before уже это пятно functions as an emphatic modifier meaning 'this one/just this' (one single stain); уже here has an intensifying/temporal sense 'already'; отнимет is the future tense ('will take away') of отнять; у меня uses the preposition у + genitive to indicate 'from me' (the one who loses something); девять десятых is the numerical expression 'nine-tenths' (governing genitive), собственного достоинства means 'of my own dignity.' Word order places the emphatic 'одно' and 'уже' before 'это пятно' and the verb before the phrase indicating what is lost 'у меня'.
segments.p2-3-g574: Знал тоже я places the past verb (знал 'knew') before the pronoun for stylistic emphasis; тоже ('also/too') signals that this knowledge is in addition to something previously mentioned. The inversion (verb before subject) is a common stylistic device in Russian narration.
segments.p2-3-g575: This subordinate clause (что ...) states the content of that knowledge: очень ('very') modifies низко ('low/basely') which here has a moral sense 'base' or 'beneath one' (i.e., disgraceful), так ('so/that way') modifies думать (infinitive 'to think'). The infinitive functions as the clause's predicate: 'that to think so is very low.'
segments.p2-3-g553: Я is the nominative subject 'I'. собственноручно is an adverb meaning 'with one's own hands / personally' modifying the verb. ещё раз is a fixed combination meaning 'once more', and вычистил is the perfective past tense (masculine singular) 'polished/cleaned thoroughly'; мои сапоги is the accusative object 'my boots'.
segments.p2-3-g554: Аполлон is the nominative proper name. ни за что на свете is an emphatic negative idiom 'for nothing in the world / never'. не стал бы + infinitive (чистить) forms the conditional 'would not (have) cleaned'; их is the object pronoun 'them' (the boots). два раза в день = 'twice a day' (раза here = 'times', в день = 'per day').
segments.p2-3-g555: находя is a present active participle (деепричастие) from находить and here functions parenthetically as 'finding/considering', giving the reason for the preceding clause.
segments.p2-3-g556: что introduces the subordinate clause 'that…'. это is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/it' as the subject of the clause, and не порядок is a negated nominal predicate meaning 'not in order / not proper' — together 'finding that this was not proper.'
segments.p2-3-g557: Чистил is the past imperfective form 'cleaned/was cleaning' used here to narrate the action; же is an emphatic/contrastive particle (adds 'however' or emphasis); word order (verb before subject) stresses the action. я is the subject 'I'.
segments.p2-3-g558: украв is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) 'having stolen', marking an action prior to the main verb. щетки is the accusative plural 'brushes'; из + genitive передней = 'from the передняя' (the front room/entrance-hall — передняя here refers to the hall).
