segments.p2-3-g559: чтоб is a purpose conjunction 'so that / in order that'. он is the subject 'he'. как-нибудь (как + нибудь) is an adverbial softener meaning 'somehow / in any way'. не заметил is past perfective negative 'would not notice' in the purpose clause; и не стал потом презирать меня is coordinated: не стал (стал + бы implied) 'would not begin' + infinitive презирать 'to despise', потом 'later', меня = 'me' — together 'so that he wouldn't notice in any way and later would not begin to despise me.'
segments.p2-3-g560: Затем is an adverb 'then/afterwards'. я is the subject. подробно modifies the verb as 'in detail / carefully'. осмотрел is the perfective past 'inspected/looked over'. мое платье is the object 'my garment/clothes'. и нашёл 'and found' introduces the following reported content clause.
segments.p2-3-g561: что is the conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause. все is a pronoun 'everything' (here neuter aggregate), and старо is a short-form neuter predicate 'old' agreeing with все ('everything is old').
segments.p2-3-g562: потерто is a short-form adjective/participle meaning 'worn' or 'scuffed' and functions as a predicative adjective in the series describing the clothes.
segments.p2-3-g563: заношено is another short-form predicate describing the state of the clothes — 'worn out/hand-me-down' (literally 'worn by being worn'); it completes the triad of negative qualities.
segments.p2-3-g564: Слишком is an adverb 'too/excessively'. я is the subject. уж is an emphatic particle ('already' / intensifying). обнеряшился is a colloquial perfective reflexive verb meaning 'became slovenly / let myself go' — the whole phrase means 'I have become far too untidy/slovenly.'
segments.p2-3-g599: 'Это-то' is emphatic — the demonstrative 'это' plus the clitic '-то' gives 'this very / that very (thing)'. 'Но' is a simple adversative opener. 'Уж' is an emphatic particle here ('indeed'/'really'), and 'было' is the neuter past of 'быть' used impersonally with the predicate 'невозможно' (it was impossible). The phrase 'больше всего' literally 'more than anything' means 'above all'.
segments.p2-3-g600: 'Уж когда...' opens a subordinate temporal clause: 'once/when'. 'Меня начинало тянуть' is an impersonal-locutional construction meaning literally 'it began to pull me' but functionally 'I began to feel drawn (toward it)'; 'начинало' is past imperfective (began to) and 'тянуть' is the infinitive 'to pull/attract'.
segments.p2-3-g601: The double 'так уж ... так и' is a colloquial intensifying repetition: 'so indeed ... just so I ...'. 'Я' is the subject. 'Втягивался' is the past imperfective reflexive form meaning 'was being drawn in / I would be drawn in' (repeated/habitual or reflexive sense). 'Весь' here is an adverbial intensifier meaning 'completely/all over'.
segments.p2-3-g602: The prepositional phrase 'с головой' is idiomatic: literally 'with (one's) head' but used adverbially to mean 'headfirst' or 'completely'.
segments.p2-3-g603: 'Я бы' with the past-tense verb 'дразнил' forms a conditional/hypothetical 'I would (do)'. 'Всю жизнь' is a temporal object 'all my life / my whole life', fronted for emphasis. 'Себя' is the reflexive object 'myself'. 'Потом' places the action in retrospective time: 'afterward/then'.
segments.p2-3-g604: In direct speech 'А что,' is an idiomatic exclamatory opener (colloquial 'Well, what/And so'), here introducing the taunt. 'А' functions as a discourse particle and 'что' is the interrogative/exclamatory 'what/that'.
segments.p2-3-g605: 'Струсил' is the masculine past tense of 'струсить' meaning 'chickened out / took fright'; here it's quoted direct speech, a blunt accusatory past form.
segments.p2-3-g606: The repeated 'струсил' is followed by 'действительности' in the genitive: 'струсил действительности' = 'chickened out in the face of/re before reality' — the genitive marks the object/thing he flinched from. The rhythm repeats the verb for emphasis.
segments.p2-3-g607: A final exclamatory repetition of the past-tense verb in the quoted taunt; punctuation marks the speaker's self-reproach.
segments.p2-3-g608: 'Напротив' is an adverbial discourse marker meaning 'on the contrary' or 'quite the opposite', introducing the opposite position to the previous thought.
segments.p2-3-g609: 'Мне хотелось' is an impersonal dative construction meaning 'I felt like / I wanted' (literally 'it was desired to me'). 'Страстно' is an adverb 'passionately/intensely'. 'Доказать' is the infinitive 'to prove'. 'Всей этой шушере' is dative singular ('to all this riffraff') — 'всей' + demonstrative 'этой' modify the noun 'шушере' (dative of 'шушера').
segments.p2-3-g610: Subordinate clause introduced by 'что' ('that'). 'Вовсе не' is a strong negation meaning 'by no means / not at all'. 'Такой трус' = 'such a coward' (comparative 'such/that kind of').
segments.p2-3-g611: 'Как' here is a comparative 'as / like'. 'Я сам себе' stacks reflexive/emphatic pronouns: 'I myself to myself' (emphatic and reflexive), and 'представляю' is present tense 'I imagine / I picture'. The phrase as a whole: 'as I myself imagine (myself)'.
segments.p2-3-g612: 'Мало того' is an idiom meaning 'moreover / what's more'. 'Мало' literally 'little' plus 'того' yields the connective 'more than that'.
segments.p2-3-g613: The prepositional phrase 'в самом сильнейшем пароксизме' uses superlative/adverbial force: 'in the very strongest paroxysm'. 'Трусливой лихорадки' is a genitive attributive 'of cowardly fever'. 'Мне мечталось' is impersonal/dative past meaning 'I dreamed (it seemed to me) / I longed' — 'мечталось' governs an infinitive clause; 'одержать верх' is an idiom 'to obtain the upper hand'.
segments.p2-3-g614: A series of infinitives follows the verb of desire; 'победить' is an infinitive meaning 'to defeat / to win', parallel to the other verbs.
segments.p2-3-g615: 'Увлечь' is another infinitive in the series meaning 'to captivate / to carry away (figuratively: to charm or win over)'; here it complements the speaker's imagined victories over the others.
segments.p2-3-g616: 'Заставить их полюбить себя' is a causative construction: 'to force/make them love me' — 'заставить' + accusative object 'их' + infinitive 'полюбить' + reflexive 'себя' ('love me'). The dash and quoted phrase introduced by 'ну хоть' ('well, at least') give a minimal, sarcastic example of the motive: 'за' here means 'for' (i.e., to admire me for my loftiness of thought and unmistakable wit).
segments.p2-3-g647: Наконец is an adverb meaning “at last” or “finally,” placed at the start to mark the culmination. На моих дрянных стенных часишках is a prepositional phrase (на + prepositional plural) meaning “on my shabby little wall clock(s),” where моих and дрянных agree with стенных часишках. Прошипело пять is a past-tense, neuter, singular verb phrase: прошипело is a perfective past onomatopoetic verb “hissed/ hissed out,” and пять (a numeral, grammatically treated as neuter singular here) is the subject — hence the neuter past form of the verb.
segments.p2-3-g648: Я схватил шапку и is a simple main clause: Я (I) is the subject; схватил is the masculine past tense of схватить (“grabbed”); шапку is the direct object in accusative (“my hat”); и (“and”) links this clause to the following participial/parenthetical construction.
segments.p2-3-g649: стараясь не взглянуть на Аполлона is a déeprichastie (gerund) clause expressing a simultaneous attempt: стараясь (present gerund of стараться) = “trying/endeavouring”; не взглянуть is the perfective infinitive “not to glance/look”; на + Аполлона marks the object of the glance (“at Apollon”). The gerund gives manner/circumstance for the main action.
segments.p2-3-g650: - который еще с утра все ждал от меня выдачи жалованья, is a relative clause modifying Аполлона: который = “who”; ещё с утра = “since/starting from the morning” (ещё + с утра conveying that he had been awaiting all morning); всё ждал here conveys continual/ongoing waiting (ждал = past tense “waited/was waiting”); от меня выдачи жалованья = “for me to give/pay out the wages” (от with the genitive меня marks from whom, выдачи is the noun “issue/handing out,” жалованья = “wages/salary”). The verb ждал agrees with the masculine antecedent.
segments.p2-3-g651: но по гордости своей не хотел заговорить первый, is a continuation of the relative clause giving the reason: но = “but”; по гордости своей is an idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning “out of his pride” (по + noun + possessive); не хотел заговорить = “would not want to begin speaking” (хотел is past tense ‘wanted/would’ with negative giving ‘would not’); первый = “first” (to be the first to speak).
segments.p2-3-g652: - скользнул мимо него из дверей и на лихаче, has the narrator’s main motion: скользнул is past tense perfective “slipped/past slipped” (masculine); мимо него = “past/by him” (мимо + accusative); из дверей = “out of the door(s)” (из + genitive); и на лихаче uses на with a colloquial noun лихач/лихаче here indicating the reckless rider/driver (i.e. “on the daredevil” / “on the hired daredevil”), introducing the vehicle/person the narrator mounts or takes.
segments.p2-3-g653: которого нарочно нанял за последний полтинник, is a relative clause modifying лихаче: которого = “whom/that” (accusative relative pronoun); нарочно = “deliberately/on purpose”; нанял = past tense “hired” (I hired); за последний полтинник = “for the last half-ruble” (за marking payment, последний = “last,” полтинник = 50-kopeck coin). The clause explains that the narrator had purposely paid the last of his money to hire this reckless driver.
segments.p2-3-g654: подкатил барином к Нotel de Paris. is the concluding action: подкатил is past tense perfective “pulled up/approached” (masculine); барином is instrumental singular of барин (“landlord/master/sir”) used adverbially to express manner — “like a gentleman/master”; к + Hotel de Paris = “to/at the Hotel de Paris” (к + dative). Together: he drew up like a gentleman at the Hotel de Paris.
segments.p2-3-g655: This is a marginal gloss/footnote fragment; the asterisk marks it as a footnote. Право владельца is a noun phrase in nominative + genitive ('the right of the owner' / 'owner's right') with владельца in the genitive singular. (Франц.) in parentheses is an abbreviation indicating the language (French). The segment is a label, not a full clause.
segments.p2-3-g617: 'Они' is the nominative plural pronoun 'they' placed before the verb as the subject; 'бросят' is future simple (perfective) 3rd-person plural meaning 'will abandon/throw off' here, and 'Зверкова' is the accusative form of the name Zverkov (object). The clause literally: 'They will abandon Zverkov.'
segments.p2-3-g618: 'он будет сидеть' is a periphrastic future: 'будет' (future of 'to be') + infinitive 'сидеть' = 'he will sit' or 'he will be sitting.' 'в стороне' is a prepositional phrase ('to/at the side'), placed after the verb as a locative complement.
segments.p2-3-g619: Two coordinated infinitives: 'молчать' and 'стыдиться' follow and describe simultaneous states/actions ('to be silent and to be ashamed'). In Russian, infinitives can describe ongoing or habitual actions connected to the preceding 'он будет сидеть'.
segments.p2-3-g620: The conjunction 'а' contrasts the speaker's action with the previous clause. 'я' is the nominative 'I', and 'раздавлю' is the perfective future 1st-person singular 'I will crush/overwhelm' with 'Зверкова' as its accusative object, repeating the name for emphasis.
segments.p2-3-g621: 'Потом' is an adverb meaning 'later' or 'afterwards' and here sets the temporal frame at the start of the sentence.
segments.p2-3-g622: 'пожалуй' is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose', softening the assertion that follows and making it tentative.
segments.p2-3-g623: 'помирюсь' is perfective future 1st person singular ('I will reconcile'); 'с ним' is the preposition 'с' plus the pronoun in the instrumental 'him' meaning 'with him'; 'и выпью' coordinates another future action ('and I will drink'); 'на ты' is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'to switch to the informal 'ты' form' — 'на' marks the change of state or purpose.
segments.p2-3-g624: 'но' is the adversative conjunction 'but'; 'что' introduces the content clause ('what/that'); 'всего' here functions with a superlative sense ('of all [things]'); 'было' is past neuter of 'to be' used impersonally; 'злее и обиднее' are comparative adjectives ('crueler/more spiteful and more offensive/hurtful'); 'для меня' is a dative phrase 'for me'.
segments.p2-3-g625: 'это' is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/it' used to pick out the clause that follows as the thing being described (i.e., 'what it was').
segments.p2-3-g626: 'что' introduces the subordinate clause; 'я' is the subject; 'тогда же' combines 'then' with the emphatic particle 'же' to mean 'right then' or 'immediately then'; 'знал' is past tense masculine singular 'knew'.
segments.p2-3-g627: The repeated 'знал' reiterates knowledge; 'вполне' ('completely') and 'наверно' ('certainly/surely') are adverbial modifiers following the verb to intensify the speaker's certainty.
segments.p2-3-g628: This fragment begins a nested clause: 'что' introduces it; 'ничего' ('nothing') is the pronoun, 'мне' is dative 'to me', and 'этого' is the genitive demonstrative 'of this' — together roughly 'that of this I wanted nothing' (i.e., 'I didn't want any of it').
segments.p2-3-g629: 'в сущности' is a parenthetical phrase meaning 'in essence' or 'essentially', qualifying the following negation and softening it as a general statement about his true attitude.
segments.p2-3-g630: The impersonal/elliptical phrase 'не надо' means 'is not necessary' or idiomatically 'I don't need it'; 'не' negates the modal 'надо'.
segments.p2-3-g631: 'что' again introduces another explanatory clause ('that...'), linking the thought to what precedes.
segments.p2-3-g632: Another parenthetical 'в сущности' reaffirming 'in essence' or 'actually' before restating his inner attitude.
segments.p2-3-g633: 'я' is subject; 'вовсе не' is a strong negation 'not at all'; 'желаю' is present tense 1st person 'I wish/desire'; the infinitive 'их раздавливать' ('to crush them') is the object of 'желать' with 'их' (them) as the accusative pronoun. The imperfective infinitive 'раздавливать' indicates a general action (to be crushing/overwhelming) rather than a one-off completed act.
segments.p2-3-g634: The single infinitive 'покорять' continues the coordinated list of actions he does not wish to perform; here it carries the sense 'to subdue / to win over' rather than physical 'conquer' in a military sense.
segments.p2-3-g635: 'привлекать' is another coordinated infinitive meaning 'to attract / to win over'; the conjunction 'и' links it to what follows, and 'что за весь-то результат' begins a new clause where 'за' has the sense 'for' or 'as for', 'весь-то' ('the whole' + emphatic particle 'то') stresses 'the whole result', and 'результат' is the noun 'result/outcome'.
segments.p2-3-g636: 'если б' is the conditional particle 'if (would)', with 'б' marking the counterfactual; 'только' here means 'even if only' or 'just'; 'я' is subject; 'и' is emphatic ('even'); 'достиг' is past tense perfective 'had reached/attained'; 'его' is accusative/pronoun referring back to 'it' (the result). Together: 'even if I had achieved it.'
segments.p2-3-g637: 'я сам' places emphasis on the subject: 'I myself' (the reflexive/emphatic use 'сам' here stresses personal responsibility or primacy).
segments.p2-3-g638: 'первый' here is used parenthetically as 'the first' (i.e., 'I myself, first of all' or 'I would be the first to...'), reinforcing that he would lead in withholding any reward.
segments.p2-3-g639: This is a conditional clause with 'бы' marking irreality: 'гроша' is the genitive of 'грош' (a small coin) used idiomatically after negation to mean 'not a penny'; 'бы' is the conditional particle and 'не дал' is past tense of 'to give' negated — together 'would not give a penny.'
segments.p2-3-g640: О is an exclamatory particle ("Oh!") used to introduce an emotional address or exclamation; here it simply adds strong feeling before the following clause.
segments.p2-3-g641: Как я молил бога is an exclamatory past-tense clause: как = "how" (introducing an exclamation), я молил is past first-person sing. of молить (to implore/pray), and бога is the direct object in the accusative (God) — "how I begged/prayed to God."
segments.p2-3-g642: Чтоб introduces a desiderative/subjunctive clause ("that ..." / "so that ...") and takes a past form to express a wish; уж is an intensifier meaning roughly "already/at last/just," прошел is the past form of пройти used optatively here ("would pass/finish"), поскорее is a comparative adverb meaning "more quickly/sooner," and этот день is the lexical object placed at the end for emphasis: "that this day would just pass more quickly."
segments.p2-3-g643: В невыразимой тоске is a prepositional adverbial phrase ('in inexpressible melancholy') — невыразимой is the prepositional (feminine singular) form of the adjective and agrees with тоске (prepositional of тоска). The verb подходил is past imperfective, here conveying repeated or habitual past action ('I would approach' / 'I kept going'), with я as the subject. К окну uses the preposition к + dative (окну) to indicate movement toward the window.
segments.p2-3-g644: Отворял форточку и вглядывался binds two imperfective past actions coordinated by и, giving a sequence/habit ('opened the vent and peered'). Отворял is an older/literary imperfective verb meaning 'opened' (parallel to открывал); форточку is the accusative object (diminutive/vent window). Вглядывался is reflexive imperfective 'to peer/strain to see' and takes в + accusative (в мутную мглу) meaning 'into/at the murky gloom'. Густо падающего мокрого снега is a genitive attribute modifying мглу ('the gloom of the thickly falling wet snow'): падающего is a present active participle in genitive singular agreeing with снега, мокрого is a genitive adjective, and густо (an adverb) modifies падающего. Note the participial phrase follows the head noun, a common Russian order for such modifiers.
segments.p2-3-g576: Но (contrastive conjunction) + теперь (adverb) set the temporal/contrasting frame. The phrase не до + noun (не до думанья) is an idiom meaning 'not a time/occasion for X' — here до takes the sense 'regarding / as to' and думанья is a nominalized gerund meaning 'thinking / preoccupation'.
segments.p2-3-g577: теперь (adverb 'now') + наступает (3rd sing. present of наступать) expresses the onset of something — 'begins/sets in'. Действительность is the nominative subject following the verb for emphasis: 'now reality begins/sets in.'
segments.p2-3-g578: думал я is past-tense narration with subject placed after the verb (stylistic inversion). и падал духом: падал (past imperfective of падать) + духом (instrumental of дух used idiomatically: 'in spirit') forms the set phrase падать духом — 'to lose heart / have one's spirits sink'.
segments.p2-3-g579: Знал is past tense of знать ('knew'); the subject я follows the verb (inversion for emphasis). тоже 'also' and отлично ('very well / perfectly') modify knew: 'I also knew perfectly well.'
segments.p2-3-g580: тогда же is a temporal phrase: тогда 'then' + же (emphatic particle) yields 'right then / even at that time' — the же strengthens the immediacy.
segments.p2-3-g581: что introduces a content clause ('that ...'). The subordinate clause uses the present tense преувеличиваю (1st person) even though the matrix verb is past — a normal Russian way to report a contemporaneous mental state: 'that I was (in fact) exaggerating.' чудовищно is an adverb intensifier 'monstrously / extremely.'
segments.p2-3-g582: но что же было делать: is a rhetorical question/phrase meaning 'but what was there to be done?' что here is 'what', же adds emphasis, было (past impersonal) + делать (infinitive) forms the idiomatic expression.
segments.p2-3-g583: совладать с собой 'to master/control oneself' uses the infinitive совладать with the reflexive idea expressed by с собой. The past clause уж не мог (я уж не мог) = 'I could no longer' — уж adds emphasis/already.
segments.p2-3-g584: и меня трясла лихорадка: трясла (past feminine) agrees with subject лихорадка, which comes last for stylistic effect; меня (accusative) is the object placed before the verb for emphasis — 'and a fever was shaking me.'
segments.p2-3-g585: С отчаянием (instrumental) is an adverbial phrase 'with despair'. представлял я себе is past tense + reflexive себя: 'I pictured to myself / imagined' — verb before subject for stylistic ordering.
segments.p2-3-g586: как introduces the subordinate clause 'how ...'. свысока and холодно are adverbs ('condescendingly' and 'coldly') modifying встретит (future perfective 3rd sing. of встретить) — 'will receive/greet me'. The object меня appears before the subject for emphasis. The name Зверков is a proper name here (personal name), not the literal sense of the lemma.
segments.p2-3-g587: с каким (instrumental) + тупым (adjective in instrumental) begins a clause 'with what dull ...' — the construction с каким + adjective is used to mark the manner/quality of the forthcoming look or attitude.
segments.p2-3-g588: ничем неотразимым презрением is an instrumental noun phrase: ничем 'by nothing' + неотразимым 'irresistible' + презрением 'with contempt' gives 'with an irresistibly overpowering contempt'. будет смотреть на меня is the periphrastic future meaning 'will look/regard me', with the subject тупица Трудолюбов placed after for effect.
segments.p2-3-g589: как ... будет подхихикивать: 'how nastily and insolently (скверно и дерзко) he will titter/snicker' — подхихикивать is an iterative imperfective verb ('snicker repeatedly'); на мой счет is an idiomatic prepositional phrase 'at my expense / about me' (счёт = 'regard/consideration'). Козявка Ферфичкин is the subject (pejorative epithet + name).
segments.p2-3-g590: чтоб introduces a purpose clause 'in order that / so that'. подслужиться is reflexive infinitive 'to ingratiate oneself', and Зверкову is the dative target 'to Zverkov' (purpose: to curry favor with him).
segments.p2-3-g591: как отлично поймет про себя все это: поймет is future perfective ('will understand/realize'), про себя ('to himself/privately') indicates personal reflection, and все это is the content 'all this'. The following и ... будет презирать is a parallel clause: будет презирать (future periphrastic) 'will despise' меня 'me' за introduces the reason 'for (because of)' + genitive phrase низость моего тщеславия и малодушия 'the baseness of my vanity and cowardice.'
segments.p2-3-g592: и is a simple coordinating conjunction 'and', linking the next parenthetical remark.
segments.p2-3-g593: главное is a parenthetical neuter adjective used adverbially: 'the main thing / above all'. It introduces the primary point the narrator emphasizes.
segments.p2-3-g594: — как все это будет мизерно: dash + как introduces the summative exclamation 'how all this will be petty/tiny'. Будет (future) + predicate мизерно (adverb) describes the overall quality.
segments.p2-3-g595: не литературно is a negated adverbial phrase 'not literary / unliterary' — не negates the adverb литературно.
segments.p2-3-g596: обыденно (final adverb) closes the list with 'banal/ordinary/commonplace', completing the triad of pejorative qualities.
segments.p2-3-g597: Конечно is a sentence adverb 'of course / certainly', signaling the narrator's conclusion or obviousness.
segments.p2-3-g598: всего бы лучше is a conditional evaluative phrase: бы is the conditional particle ('would'), лучше is the comparative 'better' (here 'best of all' with всего adding generalizing emphasis). совсем не ехать is the infinitive negated 'to not go at all' (совсем 'completely/at all' + не + ехать).
